role: MP; Chairman, Majlis Cultural Committee; former Secretary-General, Front of Islamic Revolution Stability
bio: Morteza Agha-Tehrani is a senior figure of the [Front of Islamic Revolution Stability](/orgs/front-of-islamic-revolution-stability) who served as the party's founding Secretary-General before handing the role to [Sadegh Mahsouli](/people/sadegh-mahsouli) in late 2020. He chairs the [Islamic Consultative Assembly](/orgs/islamic-consultative-assembly)'s Cultural Committee — a key position that made him the primary legislative driver of the controversial mandatory hijab enforcement bill. He was also described as [Mahmoud Ahmadinejad](/people/mahmoud-ahmadinejad)'s personal ethics teacher, cementing the Paydari network's ties to the Ahmadinejad presidency.
editorial_notes: Birth year not publicly confirmed. Former Secretary-General of Paydari. Cultural Committee chair in 11th and 12th Majlis.
